claim: QuickAdd by chhoumann is at 2.2k GitHub stars with v2.12.0 released March 5 2026, bundling Captures, Templates, Macros, and Multi choices.
quote: QuickAdd is an Obsidian plugin combining four tools called choices: Captures, Templates, Macros, and Multi choices ... 2.2k stars ... Latest Release: Version 2.12.0 (March 5, 2026)

claim: QuickAdd's Capture choice appends content to predefined files such as appending a link to today's daily note under a specific section.
quote: A Capture allows you to quickly add content to predefined files. For example, you could set up a quick action to add a link to the open file to your daily note under a specific section.

claim: Templater by SilentVoid13 is at 4.9k GitHub stars and actively maintained with v2.20.0 released April 29 2026, supporting JS execution and dynamic variables in templates.
quote: GitHub Stars: 4.9k ... latest release: 2.20.0 on Apr 29, 2026 ... defines a templating language that lets you insert variables and functions results into your notes

claim: Templater can bind a template to a hotkey via the Template Hotkeys setting, hidden until 'Trigger Template on new file creation' is enabled.
quote: Template Hotkeys allows you to bind a template to a hotkey. This setting is hidden by default. To view it first enable the Trigger Template on new file creation setting.

claim: Periodic Notes (liamcain) sits at 1.3k stars but its last public release is the 1.0.0-beta.3 from April 2022 and the community widely treats it as effectively unmaintained.
quote: 1.3k stars and 107 forks ... The latest release (1.0.0-beta.3) is dated April 14, 2022

claim: The Journals plugin (srg-kostyrko) is the actively-maintained 2025/2026 replacement for Periodic Notes, supporting daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly plus interval-based journals and offering bulk import from Periodic Notes.
quote: deactivated the calendar plugin, Periodic Notes & Daily Notes and everything seems to work with the Journals plugin only

claim: Sébastien Dubois's 2026 must-have list pairs QuickAdd for capture with Templater for note typing, plus Rollover Daily Todos and Auto Note Mover for inbox processing.
quote: design clearly defined note types, then templates for each of those making it much easier to be systematic in the way you take notes

claim: The dominant power-user pattern is QuickAdd plus the Global Hotkeys plugin, optionally augmented with Alfred/ULauncher, with Ctrl+Alt+A as a common trigger.
quote: I use both: I use the App Launcher when I am doing focused work, and the global hotkey whenever I don't mind switching contexts to provide a little more organization.

claim: On Windows, an AutoHotkey script bound to Ctrl+Alt+V activates Obsidian if running (or launches it) then sends Ctrl+Alt+C to open QuickAdd's capture window for system-wide capture.
quote: IfWinExist, ahk_exe Obsidian.exe...WinActivate, Obsidian.exe ... checks if the program is already running

claim: A widely-shared inbox-processing pattern combines Advanced URI, Templater, and Hotkeys for Templates with Opt+A/D/M bindings for archive, trash, and move on each captured note.
quote: I was putting some of my fleeting ideas in a system that would never be resurfaced

claim: Thino (1.3k stars, v3.0.10 April 2026) is a Twitter-style quick-capture surface that posts time-stamped entries into the daily note, but went closed-source from v2.0.0 with paid pro features.
quote: From version 2.0.0 onward, Thino is closed-source ... the input transforms into formatted entries like '- 22:15 {your input content}' within daily notes

claim: The dominant Obsidian inbox idiom is to treat today's daily note as the universal capture target and process it during review rather than at capture time.
quote: Daily notes act as a central inbox and daily dashboard ... Today's note becomes your inbox, and processing happens during review time rather than during capture.

claim: Obsidian Mobile 1.12 (Feb 27 2026) added a native iOS Share extension to save content to the vault from other apps without opening Obsidian, plus a 'Bookmark Link' Shortcut action and a 'Unique note' app-icon long-press action.
quote: iOS: New Share extension to save content to your vault from other apps (e.g. Safari, social networks) without opening Obsidian. iOS: New Shortcut action 'Bookmark Link' to save URLs as Obsidian bookmarks.

claim: Obsidian Mobile 1.11 (Jan 2026) shipped Lock Screen, Control Center, and Home Screen widgets on iOS plus Android home-screen widgets, a Quick Settings Tile, and Siri/Shortcuts 'Capture to a note' that appends or prepends without launching the app.
quote: Lock Screen and Control Center widgets let you quickly Create a new note, Open a specific note, Open daily note, Open search, or Open Obsidian... New shortcuts: Open a note, Open daily note, Capture to a note. Siri: 'Capture using Obsidian'.

claim: Obsidian's public roadmap lists Mobile widgets, Siri/Shortcuts integration, Mobile UI refresh, and Mobile startup-time speed-ups as in-flight items, but no dedicated 'quick capture' entry remains beyond what shipped in 1.11/1.12.
quote: Mobile widgets — Quick actions from your lock screen, home screen, and more. Siri and Shortcuts integration — Support for opening and editing notes using Siri and Shortcuts iOS. Mobile startup time — Speed improvements for opening and editing notes on mobile.

claim: The 2026 Obsidian Report Card scores mobile 3.1/5 and notes the architectural problem that even widget/Shortcut taps still load the full vault, with users describing capture as 'still awkward and kludgy' despite 1.11/1.12 improvements.
quote: After you tap the widget icon, the full Obsidian app opens and loads your entire vault... 'Capturing to Obsidian on mobile is still awkward and kludgy'... 'Despite recent improvements to speed and quick capture, the friction is still to high'.

claim: Carlo Zottmann's Actions for Obsidian (iOS 18.4+, free) exposes 40+ dedicated Shortcuts actions — append, prepend, create from template, fetch frontmatter — making it a first-class building block for iOS Shortcuts/Action-button capture chains.
quote: Actions For Obsidian brings the power of 40+ dedicated Shortcuts actions to your iOS device, making Obsidian a first-class citizen in Apple's Shortcuts app.

claim: Quick Draft is a free Android/iOS companion app for Obsidian supporting text/voice/image capture with high-quality transcription, OCR, custom date-formatted file paths, template wrapping, share-sheet integration, and iOS Siri shortcuts; AI title generation is paid.
quote: Quick Draft is a free mobile companion app... Voice recording with high-quality transcription... Customizable file paths using date formatting (e.g., /YYYY/[W]-w/YYYY-MM-DD)... Widgets and iOS Siri shortcuts for rapid access.

claim: Drafts (Greg Pierce) since v28's Bookmarks feature can write Markdown files directly into an iCloud-stored Obsidian vault on iOS without opening Obsidian, making Drafts → Obsidian one of the most reliable lock-screen-fast capture flows.
quote: Thanks to the Bookmarks feature added in Drafts version 28, you can now store text outside of the Drafts iOS sandbox... the most reliable place to store a vault to be able to use file-based actions on iOS is in your iCloud Drive folder.

claim: Forum consensus on Android is that Tasker can drive Obsidian capture either via AutoInput/AutoShare plugins or — more reliably — by writing a timestamped Markdown file directly into the vault folder, with the caveat that sync only fires when Obsidian is opened.
quote: Just have Tasker write a file in the Vault folder of your choice!... the Vault sync only happens when Obsidian is opened, it seems.

claim: Harshil Chordia's open-source Android home-screen widget (github.com/harshilchordia/obsidian-widget, ~14 stars) renders pinned notes with interactive checkboxes and a Quick Capture button that appends to today's daily note without opening Obsidian.
quote: Pinned notes: show today's daily note or pick specific files. Interactive checkboxes — tap to toggle - [ ] / - [x]. Quick capture: Captures text and stores it as daily note in root folder.

claim: A 2025 quick-capture writeup recommends a two-tier flow: an iOS Shortcut widget that pops a system text box and appends date + #QuickNotes tag for short thoughts, falling back to Drafts for longer captures, explicitly to avoid Obsidian's slow cold-start.
quote: For a couple of sentences, this is fine by itself... Drafts is a rock solid app for quickly capturing Markdown text without losing it... it can take some time to open, and by that time, the thought we're trying to capture might have gone.

claim: Obsidian still has no first-party Apple Watch app in 2026; community workarounds rely on Drafts' Watch complication, Siri Shortcuts that round-trip via iCloud, or third-party watchOS recorders like Uplink that drop transcribed Markdown into the vault.
quote: Obsidian doesn't have a Watch app, but there are workarounds available... Uplink is a standalone watchOS recorder that turns spoken thought into Markdown notes in your own Obsidian vault.

claim: Fleeting Notes positions itself as the Android-first answer to Obsidian's mobile-capture friction, syncing scratch notes into the vault and frequently surfacing alongside Drafts and Funnel as Reddit/forum-recommended low-friction front-ends.
quote: Fleeting Notes... was developed specifically to fix the Quick Capture problem in Obsidian.

claim: Obsidian Web Clipper is the official cross-browser extension from the Obsidian team, sits at 4.1k GitHub stars, latest release 1.6.2 (April 23, 2026) with 65 published versions.
quote: Highlight and capture the web in your favorite browser. The official Web Clipper extension for Obsidian.

claim: The official Web Clipper saves to local Markdown, supports highlighting, includes pre-built templates for articles, recipes, references and academic papers, and runs on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge, Brave, Arc, Orion and Vivaldi.
quote: Highlight and capture web pages in your favorite browser. Save anything and everything with just one click.

claim: Web Clipper's Interpreter feature sends page context plus template prompts to a chosen LLM (BYO key — Claude, GPT, Gemini, or local) and returns values into template variables using the {{"prompt"}} syntax.
quote: When you click interpret, Interpreter sends the page context to your selected model, along with all the prompts in your template in one request.

claim: After Omnivore's shutdown, Obsidian users coalesced around the Web Clipper for archival clipping plus Raindrop.io as a triage inbox, with Raindrop's caveat that it does not store full content as Markdown.
quote: No full content saved in Markdown, as Omnivore was doing.

claim: Omnivore shut down in November 2024 after the team was acquihired by ElevenLabs; the obsidian-omnivore plugin's API is offline and previously synced notes remain but no new sync is possible.
quote: Omnivore shut down in November 2024 after its team was acquihired by ElevenLabs. The service is permanently offline and all user data has been deleted.

claim: The official Readwise Obsidian plugin auto-exports highlights from Kindle, Apple Books, Instapaper, Pocket, Medium, Twitter, PDFs and Readwise Reader into the vault with Jinja2 templating and continuous sync.
quote: Official Readwise plugin for Obsidian

claim: Readwise V2 of the Obsidian plugin added mobile sync (iOS/Android) and a Readwise MCP server in 2025 so AI tools (Raycast, Claude) can query highlights directly.
quote: Readwise released an MCP (Model Context Protocol), which can be used to connect other AI tools like Raycast AI with their new MCP feature.

claim: MarkDownload is a non-Obsidian-specific Firefox/Chrome/Edge/Safari extension that converts pages to Markdown files; it can write into a vault folder via the Advanced Obsidian URI plugin to bypass URL-length limits.
quote: A Firefox and Google Chrome extension to clip websites and download them into a readable markdown file.

claim: The Matter Obsidian plugin (128 GitHub stars, last release v1.1.4 November 2022) syncs Matter highlights and notes via QR-code pairing, but the plugin appears inactive with 19 open issues.
quote: The plugin synchronizes all of your Matter highlights and notes directly to your Obsidian vault.

claim: Matter Premium ($60/yr in 2026) is the closest still-maintained equivalent to Omnivore's free tier, bundling newsletter sync, RSS, AI Co-Reader and unlimited Obsidian/Notion highlight export.
quote: Matter Premium at $60/year adds HD text-to-speech, AI Co-Reader, newsletter sync, RSS feeds, unlimited highlights with Notion/Obsidian export.

claim: The Obsidian Hypothesis community plugin syncs Hypothes.is web annotations into the vault on startup or manually, with Nunjucks template formatting.
quote: An Obsidian.md plugin that syncs highlights from Hypothesis.

claim: The lindylearn obsidian-annotations fork adds bi-directional Hypothes.is sync so vault edits propagate back to the web annotations.
quote: Obsidian plugin to sync highlights from Hypothesis bidirectionally.

claim: Email2Obsidian provides a unique forwarding address that buffers messages 72 hours then a vault plugin pulls subjects, bodies, and attachments; free tier covers 10 lifetime emails, Starter is $2.49/mo for 300 monthly emails.
quote: Free Tier: No credit card needed - 10 lifetime emails, Full plugin access with community support

claim: TaskRobin Email Sync archives forwarded emails (and attachments) into Obsidian via a secure forwarding alias, aimed at users who want to keep email artifacts inside the vault.
quote: TaskRobin Email Sync for Obsidian allows users to archive emails and attachments directly into their vault.

claim: The popular RSS Reader plugin by joethei (468 stars) was archived August 25, 2025 with last release v1.2.2 from June 2022, leaving Simple RSS, Local RSS and RSS Dashboard as the actively maintained replacements.
quote: Archived - The repository was archived by the owner on August 25, 2025, and is now read-only.

claim: RSS Dashboard is an actively maintained alternative that aggregates RSS, YouTube channels and podcasts with smart tagging and embedded media playback inside Obsidian.
quote: A dashboard for organizing and consuming RSS feeds, YouTube channels, and podcasts with smart tagging, media playback, and seamless content flow.

claim: Self-hosted Wallabag is the recommended privacy-conscious Omnivore replacement for users who want full data ownership rather than another SaaS.
quote: Wallabag is a stable, proven self-hosted read-later app used by thousands of privacy-conscious readers.

claim: nikdanilov/whisper-obsidian-plugin is the most-starred dedicated Whisper plugin for Obsidian at 350 stars, latest release v1.9.1 in April 2026, supporting OpenAI/Groq/Azure/Ollama/LM Studio backends, hotkey recording (Alt+Q), LLM post-processing, and URL automation for iOS Shortcuts.
quote: record or upload audio, transcribe with Whisper, and optionally post-process the result with an LLM ... Works with multiple Whisper-compatible APIs including OpenAI, Groq, Azure, and self-hosted options like Ollama and LM Studio.

claim: djmango/obsidian-transcription has 222 stars and supports both local Whisper ASR and cloud transcription with ffmpeg-based audio handling and timestamp markers, but its last release (v3.4.0) dates from April 2024 — clearly less actively maintained than nikdanilov in 2026.
quote: Whisper ASR (local, open-source) ... OpenAI Whisper integration ... Latest Release: Version 3.4.0 (April 12, 2024) ... 222 stars

claim: Smart Memos (Mossy1022, 79 stars, last release Sept 2024) is OpenAI-API-only with customizable LLM prompts for transcript+summary+expansion in one pass, with local-model support still on the roadmap.
quote: This plugin currently uses online openAI models to recieve and transcribe your voice memos ... Local Model Transcription is listed as a high priority

claim: Hands-on testing of nikdanilov's plugin shows OpenAI Whisper API costs ~$1 per 2h45m of audio with strong accent handling, while local whisper.cpp gives reduced accuracy, occasional accent failures, and needs ~16GB RAM and meaningful CPU.
quote: Costs approximately $1 per 2 hours 45 minutes of audio ... Reduced accuracy compared to the API version ... Occasional difficulty recognizing accents ... Requires approximately 16GB RAM

claim: Whisper Large-v3 hits 2.7% WER on clean English but degrades to 8–12% WER on real-world English meetings/podcasts, and accented English (Indian, Scottish) lands at 8–15% WER — a 5–10 percentage point penalty over standard.
quote: 2.7% WER on LibriSpeech test-clean ... 8–12% WER on real-world English ... Accented English (Indian, Scottish) ... 8–15% WER depending on accent strength

claim: The 2026 Obsidian forum consensus on voice-to-Obsidian is a four-stage pipeline (voice memo → transcription → AI summary → structured notes → vault), with friction concentrated around fragmented recordings from interruptions rather than transcription quality.
quote: Voice memo → transcription → AI summary → structured notes → Obsidian ... Interruptions (phone calls, Bluetooth switching) fragment single thoughts into multiple recordings

claim: On iPhone, the dominant DIY pattern is iOS Shortcuts plus RoutineHub-shared shortcuts that pipe Voice Memos through Whisper (cloud or local via Aiko) and ChatGPT formatting, then drop output into iCloud where the Obsidian vault picks it up.
quote: Uses 'openai whisper' for transcription and ChatGPT for formatting, then saves files to iCloud ... Aiko runs 'whisper entirely offline on my iphone so that the data doesn't leave my device' ... iOS Shortcuts: Apple's built-in automation platform

claim: Apple added on-device transcription to Voice Memos in iOS 18, processing locally on iPhone 12+, achieving roughly 85–90% accuracy on clear single-speaker audio but degrading with multiple speakers, length, or formatting needs.
quote: Starting with iOS 18, Apple added automatic transcription to the Voice Memos app ... Apple tries to process transcripts directly on your device ... 85-90% accuracy on clear, single-speaker recordings

claim: The AudioPen→Obsidian bridge (jonashaefele, 7 stars, last release Dec 2024) uses AudioPen webhooks with templated variables ({title},{body},{orig_transcript}) — a niche but established pattern for users who already pay for AudioPen's polishing engine.
quote: synchronizes audio transcriptions between AudioPen (or VoiceNotes) and Obsidian ... Users configure webhook URLs in AudioPen's integration settings ... Version 0.2.0 was released on December 23, 2024

claim: Vox (vincentbavitz, 78 stars) auto-watches an unprocessed folder and transcribes via the developer's free remote server (100 transcriptions/day), with filename-prefix tokens for importance/category metadata — a hands-off alternative for users who don't want to manage API keys.
quote: monitors a designated 'unprocessed' folder, transcribes audio files, and saves the transcribed text with metadata and tags ... 100 transcriptions/day limit per vault ... Files are only held in memory as buffers and are not saved to disk

claim: Wispr Flow positions itself as a system-wide voice keyboard for Obsidian claiming 220 wpm vs 45 wpm typing (~4x), cross-platform sync across Mac/Windows/iOS/Android, and natural-style adaptation rather than literal dictation.
quote: 4x faster than typing (220 words per minute vs. 45 wpm for keyboard input) ... Cross-platform: Available on Mac, Windows, iPhone, and Android with synced settings

claim: AI Tagger Universe v1.0.17 (March 2026, 84 GitHub stars) supports batch tagging entire folders or whole vault, generates nested hierarchical tags into frontmatter, and integrates with 15+ providers including Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Bedrock, Vertex AI.
quote: Batch tag entire folders or your whole vault... Local LLMs: Ollama, LM Studio, LocalAI... Cloud Services: OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Groq, Grok, Mistral, DeepSeek, Cohere, GLM (Zhipu AI), MiMo, Minimax, SiliconFlow, Aliyun, Bedrock, Vertex AI, OpenRouter

claim: Smart Connections (4.9k GitHub stars, v4.3.0 April 2026) focuses on semantic discovery via local embeddings rather than tagging or frontmatter generation, and shifted to a source-available 'Smart Plugins License' restricting competing offerings.
quote: Source available under Smart Plugins License with restrictions on general-purpose competing Obsidian offerings... The documentation makes no mention of automatic frontmatter generation or auto-tagging capabilities.

claim: Smart Connections users have raised concerns in 2026 about pricing being '10-20x typical paid Obsidian plugin pricing' and a switch to a proprietary license without notification.
quote: Users not informed about switch to proprietary license; other concerns

claim: Copilot for Obsidian (6.8k GitHub stars, v3.2.8 April 29 2026) emphasizes agentic chat, vault-wide RAG, and Composer V2 file editing rather than dedicated auto-tagging or frontmatter backfill; the Plus tier is paid via Brevilabs.
quote: The plugin emphasizes AI-assisted knowledge work rather than automated tagging systems... The search results don't indicate dedicated auto-tagging, frontmatter generation, or batch indexing features as primary functionalities.

claim: Copilot's first 2026 release added web-tab reading, YouTube/web clipper slash commands, Composer V2 with side-by-side diffs, auto-compact for 128k+ context, and Obsidian Bases support.
quote: Composer V2 editing with smarter file editing, Azure provider unification, drag-to-insert wikilinks, Obsidian Bases support, and LM Studio Responses API support

claim: Text Generator (1.9k GitHub stars, v0.8.7 April 27 2026) is a template-driven plugin where reusable prompts plus frontmatter-configurable AI settings let users batch-generate metadata, summaries, or aliases across notes; supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, HuggingFace, local models, and gpt-5/thinking models.
quote: Template Engine: Users can create reusable templates to streamline repetitive tasks, enabling systematic content generation across multiple notes... Frontmatter Configuration: The tool leverages frontmatter for customizable settings

claim: Note Companion (formerly File Organizer 2000; 832 stars, v3.6.18 April 27 2026) provides folder/tag/title/template suggestions plus an inbox-automation mode that processes raw captures in batch, and offers cloud subscription, self-host, or BYO-key access; desktop-only.
quote: Folder, tag, title, and template suggestions; Optional inbox automation for raw notes... Available as Obsidian plugin (desktop-only) with cloud subscription option, self-hosting support, or bring-your-own-API-keys approach

claim: Metadata Auto Classifier (52 stars, v1.12.9 April 2026) is a 2025 entrant that uses OpenAI GPT models to auto-generate both tags and arbitrary frontmatter fields based on customizable classification rules.
quote: AI-powered Obsidian plugin that automatically classifies and generates metadata (tags, frontmatter) for your notes... integrates with OpenAI's GPT models to suggest meaningful metadata

claim: A 2026 hands-on test paired AI Tagger Universe with Auto Note Mover and a locally-hosted Gemma 3 12B via LM Studio; the 'Generate tags for vault' command successfully retro-tagged hundreds of existing notes in under an hour of setup.
quote: running the command Generate tags for vault begins tagging all my notes automatically... organize hundreds of messy notes... entire setup completely changed how I approach note-taking

claim: The 2026 r/ObsidianMD consensus splits AI picks across three lanes rather than crowning one: Smart Composer for fastest writing assist (uses free Llama 3.2), Smart Connections for semantic search/embeddings, and Copilot for vault-wide RAG chat.
quote: Smart Composer (fastest, uses free Llama 3.2) is recommended for writing assistance, Smart Connections for semantic search and embeddings, and Copilot for vault-wide RAG chat

claim: The 2026 Obsidian-AI plugin landscape is splitting into 'retrieval engines, workflow operators, and autonomous agent surfaces', with Note Companion called out as strongest 'when the job is turning chaos into organized notes' and new entrants like Obsilo Agent (55+ tools) and Sonar (local-first) emerging.
quote: Note Companion — Positioned as strongest when the job is turning chaos into organized notes... Obsilo Agent — Described as the most ambitious current entrant, offering 55+ tools, memory layers, and agent-native capabilities

claim: LLM Tagger is a focused Ollama-only plugin that auto-applies tags to newly created or modified notes and added custom Ollama-server configuration, giving fully-local users a lighter alternative to AI Tagger Universe.
quote: LLM Tagger plugin enhances note organization in Obsidian by using locally running large language models via Ollama to automatically generate relevant tags... auto-tagging feature that applies tags to newly created or modified notes

claim: Obsidian-Frontmatter-Generator (46 stars, last updated Nov 2023) is a non-AI template-based fallback that evaluates JS/JSON expressions and includes whole-vault and per-folder commands, useful as a deterministic complement to AI tagging.
quote: Template-based... Users define a frontmatter template (JSON or JavaScript expression)... command to run in folder and in the whole vault, enabling bulk generation across multiple notes

claim: Obsidian Copilot supports both Ollama and LM Studio as local providers; Ollama needs OLLAMA_ORIGINS=app://obsidian.md* to bypass CORS, and ollama embedding models like nomic-embed-text or mxbai-embed-large enable a fully offline RAG/QA experience.
quote: Ollama has added support for local embeddings for RAG since v0.1.26! ... You can pull an embedding model such as nomic-embed-text or mxbai-embed-large ... a completely offline QA experience

claim: Smart Connections operates local-first by design — embeddings are created locally by default and notes never leave the machine unless a cloud provider is explicitly enabled.
quote: Embeddings are created locally by default. Your notes stay on your machine.

claim: Smart Connections has 786,090 community plugin downloads as of January 2026 and ~4,400 GitHub stars, signalling broad adoption among privacy-conscious Obsidian users.
quote: 786,090 downloads as of January 2026 from the official Obsidian community plugins directory and approximately 4,400 GitHub stars

claim: In 2026 Ollama's recommended generalist tagging/summarization picks are Llama 3.3 70B (86.0 MMLU, 40GB VRAM) for top quality, Qwen 2.5 32B as best mid-range all-rounder (83.2 MMLU, 20GB VRAM), and Llama 3.2 8B as the default for moderate hardware.
quote: Llama 3.3 70B for overall quality (86.0 MMLU, needs 40GB VRAM), Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B for coding (92.7% HumanEval, 20GB VRAM), and Qwen 2.5 32B as the best mid-range all-rounder (83.2 MMLU, 20GB VRAM)

claim: On 8GB-RAM machines Mistral 7B v0.3 is widely used for email replies and meeting summaries, while Llama 3.1 8B handles summarization and light coding within 6GB VRAM.
quote: Llama 3.1 8B runs on 6GB VRAM and handles conversation, summarization, light coding, and general Q&A. Mistral 7B's v0.3 update is used for email responses and meeting summaries

claim: llama.cpp crossed 100K GitHub stars in March 2026 with 700+ contributors and 15K+ forks, hitting that mark faster than PyTorch or TensorFlow.
quote: llama.cpp reached 100K stars faster than PyTorch (~7 years) or TensorFlow (~8 years), doing so in under three years

claim: Ollama wraps llama.cpp's inference backend with a friendlier shell, and crossed 110K GitHub stars by March 2026 versus ~100K for llama.cpp.
quote: Ollama wraps llama.cpp's inference backend in a simplified shell

claim: On Apple Silicon, Ollama runs through Metal GPU acceleration with no configuration; M1/M2/M3/M4 Macs achieve 15–28 tok/s on 8B models, with M3 Pro/Max reaching 28–35 tok/s on Llama 3.1 8B thanks to unified memory eliminating PCIe transfer overhead.
quote: M1/M2/M3/M4 Macs with Metal GPU acceleration achieve 15-28 tokens/second on 8B models ... M3 Pro/Max chips with 18+ GPU cores, Metal acceleration delivers 28-35 tokens/second on Llama 3.1 8B

claim: Ollama on Apple Silicon is now built on Apple's MLX framework, giving a large speedup by leveraging unified memory across CPU, GPU and Neural Engine.
quote: Ollama on Apple silicon is now built on top of Apple's MLX framework to take advantage of its unified memory architecture

claim: whisper.cpp is the C/C++ port of OpenAI Whisper maintained under ggml-org and sits at ~49,148 stars / 5,469 forks in 2026, providing offline GPU-accelerated speech-to-text.
quote: Port of OpenAI's Whisper model in C/C++

claim: Apple Intelligence Writing Tools are now partially available inside Obsidian on macOS via the Edit menu (after Electron exposed the API), requiring macOS 15.1+, an M-series Mac, and Apple Intelligence enabled.
quote: All the options under Writing Tools are greyed out even when text is selected ... Electron framework lacked native support for macOS 15.1's Writing Tools API

claim: Apple ships official Python bindings (python-apple-fm-sdk) to the on-device Foundation Models framework powering Apple Intelligence, enabling third-party tools to call the on-device LLM directly.
quote: Python bindings for access to the on-device model at the core of Apple Intelligence through the Foundation Models framework

claim: Apple's Foundation Models framework is a Swift-centric API exposing guided generation, constrained tool calling, and LoRA adapter fine-tuning on the on-device model — making it a natural backend for local Obsidian workflows on macOS/iOS.
quote: a new Swift-centric API that gives developers direct access to Apple's on-device language model, exposing guided generation, constrained tool calling, and LoRA adapter fine-tuning

claim: LM Studio in 2026 is the polished GUI competitor to Ollama, with a built-in Hugging Face model browser, side-by-side comparison, and a local OpenAI-compatible inference server reachable on http://localhost:1234/v1.
quote: LM Studio is a closed-source desktop application with a polished GUI, built-in model browser, and visual parameter controls. Its model browser connects directly to Hugging Face

claim: The privacy case for local LLMs in Obsidian is concrete — the cloud cannot see notes, the workflow runs offline, and there are no per-token costs once a model is downloaded.
quote: The cloud doesn't get access to your notes, you can use the entire system offline, and it's completely free.
